svg.text: Level 2: Decoder block detail (h=2 heads, 3 sub-layers)
Superscript j = block number. Three sub-layers vs encoder's two.
Position i only sees j ≤ i

g.text: h = σ(Ŷʲ_mid2 · Wʲ_ff1 + bʲ_1) → Fʲ_out = h · Wʲ_ff2 + bʲ_2

svg.text: Output: Yⱼ [B, n_tgt, d] → next decoder block or output head
Sub-layer 1 (self-attn): Wʲ_Q, Wʲ_K, Wʲ_V, Wʲ_O ∈ [d, d] each → 4d² params + γʲ_1, βʲ_1 ∈ ℝᵈ
Sub-layer 2 (cross-attn): Wʲ_Q_cross, Wʲ_K_cross, Wʲ_V_cross, Wʲ_O_cross → 4d² params + γʲ_2, βʲ_2 ∈ ℝᵈ
Sub-layer 3 (FFN): Wʲ_ff1 ∈ [d, d_ff], Wʲ_ff2 ∈ [d_ff, d] → 2·d·d_ff params + γʲ_3, βʲ_3 ∈ ℝᵈ
Total per decoder block: 8d² + 2·d·d_ff + 6d (vs encoder block: 4d² + 2·d·d_ff + 4d)
Difference: decoder has 4d² more parameters per block (the cross-attention W matrices + extra LayerNorm).
1. Self-attention uses causal mask (upper triangle = −∞). Encoder has no mask.
2. Extra sub-layer: cross-attention where Q comes from decoder, K/V from encoder. Score matrix is [n_tgt × n_src] rectangular.
3. Three LayerNorms per block (with 3 separate γ,β pairs) instead of encoder's two.
